bead.title: Reduce in-memory fetch aggregation copy overhead
bead.description:
Memory fetch currently builds a fresh Vec for every fetch and appends each selected record batch with extend_from_slice in Segment::read. That can reallocate and copies all fetched bytes before the FetchResponse encoder writes the response frame. Improve the in-memory fetch aggregation path without changing externally visible Kafka Fetch behavior. Prefer a small, low-risk change first: compute/capacity-plan the selected bytes and allocate once, or return a structure that avoids an intermediate copy if the trait shape already supports it. Keep retention and mid-batch fetch correctness intact.

In-scope files:
- crates/heimq/src/storage/segment.rs
- crates/heimq/src/storage/partition.rs
- crates/heimq/src/handler/fetch.rs, only if a return-shape adjustment is needed
- crates/heimq/src/storage/mod.rs, only if trait exports need adjustment

Out-of-scope:
- Durable backend changes.
- Full zero-copy protocol encoder rewrite if it requires broad trait churn.
- Fetch semantic changes for transactions or isolation level.
bead.acceptance:
1. `cargo test -p heimq storage::segment --lib` passes.
2. `cargo test -p heimq storage::partition --lib` passes.
3. `cargo test -p heimq --test integration test_rdkafka_produce_consume_roundtrip -- --ignored --nocapture` passes.
4. `BENCH_RECORDS=1000 BENCH_LATENCY_SAMPLES=10 BENCH_DIAGNOSTICS=1 cargo test -p heimq --release --test bench_baseline -- --ignored --nocapture` passes.
